notes: Created source-of-truth planning scaffold from ChatGPT consultation. No implementation or verification has started.

Locked final planning refinements: renamed M1 to Verified Ball-Side Block-Shift Evidence Spine, made Gate A/B/C hard promotion gates, selected Python analytical spine plus TypeScript replay, isolated Floodlight behind a parser port, removed spatial control from M1 acceptance, and revised result-count floor. No implementation or verification has started.
Clarified that IDSSE has no public match video, M1-M3 are coordinate replay only, and future video/timecode support is optional and conditional on rights-cleared Priori or provider assets.

Clarified that M4 does not assume Priori SDK/API/private-output access. Without access, M4 is provider adapter readiness with contracts, fixtures, and conformance tests; real Priori integration is conditional.
Re-scoped the project to end at an independent pre-meeting demo. Removed provider adapter readiness and Priori integration from planned work; M4 is now Meeting-Ready Independent Demo Package.

Clarified that M4 is the end of the project as envisioned and must deliver a polished end-to-end vertical slice with delightful UI, smooth coordinate replay, tactical animations, guided demo flow, and visual QA evidence.

Adopted planner recommendation: M1 proves truth, M2 proves query breadth, M3 proves product workflow, M4 is a conditional assistant pilot, M5 proves delight/visual QA, and M6 proves meeting-ready release reliability.

Kept six milestone names but made M4/M5 parallel after M3, made M2 capability catalog mandatory, added second-query-first rule, semantic gold sets, query trace, M4 ship/cut before M6, and no analytical changes once M5 begins.

Downloaded and source-locked the three official Figshare J03WOH raw files. Local files match official sizes and MD5 checksums; SHA-256 values are recorded in the local source manifest. Raw data and generated artifacts are local evidence and are intentionally git-ignored.

Added required verification command entrypoints. Gate A verification currently reports 21 passing source-lock checks, zero failed checks, and six not-ready downstream artifacts: canonical summary, raw parity report, data-quality report, resource report, replay bundle, and replay screenshot.

Implemented the Floodlight-backed Gate A parser boundary, canonical Parquet writer, raw XML parity checks, quality/orientation/resource reports, and a 30-second canonical replay bundle plus screenshot. Generated evidence remains local and git-ignored.
make gate-a-verify passes with 37 passing checks, zero failures, and zero not-ready checks. Gate A is not accepted yet because independent review and owner acceptance have not happened.

Owner instructed controller to proceed without independent review. Controller review accepted Gate A for proceeding to Gate B with concerns recorded: raw parity is sampled, pitch-plus-5m tolerance must be revisited in Gate B, and later TypeScript replay proof must be stronger.

Provisioned the remaining six official IDSSE matches, canonicalized all seven matches sequentially, and verified corpus invariants. Gate B verification passes with 273 passing checks, zero failures, and zero not-ready checks.

Controller review accepted Gate B for proceeding to Gate C. Independent review was not performed under the current owner-requested controller-only execution mode. Non-blocking concerns recorded: Floodlight event gameclock warnings, referee tracking excluded from tactical canonical scope, and pitch-plus-5m coordinate tolerance remains a Gate C consideration.

Implemented required tactical primitives, calibrated and froze the query on J03WOH, evaluated the unchanged query on four Fortuna matches, selected 16 accepted real moments and 8 near misses, generated evidence bundles, added the Query 1 semantic gold set, and validated the static TypeScript replay proof. A verifier-caught outcome truncation bug was fixed before acceptance.

Controller review accepted Gate C. make gate-c-verify passes with 304 Python verifier checks and zero failures after the TypeScript replay proof passes with 82 checks. Independent review was not performed under owner-requested controller-only execution mode.
M1 aggregate verification passes across Gate A, Gate B, and Gate C: 37/0/0, 273/0/0, and 304/0/0. This is controller-only verification; final owner acceptance remains pending.

Planner review accepted the architecture but rejected a single oversized M1.1 boundary. Inserted M1.1 Composable Tactical Query Runtime to prove IR, binder, generic executor, relation layer, no-code composition, traces, and M1 parity without Hermes. Inserted M1.2 Grounded Tactical Query Workshop to prove Hermes, feedback, revision diffs, and immutable recipe versions as a bounded client of M1.1. M2 is now the second approved tactical family on top of the runtime.

External planner approved the M1.1/M1.2 split with required changes. Integrated requirements: M1 baseline freeze before implementation, reviewed M1 semantic examples, stronger binder payload/cardinality/unit semantics, tri-state predicates, separated recipe/invocation/bound-plan/execution objects, narrowed geometric_progressive_corridor V1, formal EvaluationTarget non-match handling, scoped Hermes tracking-window access, quantitative M1.2 evaluation corpus, and simple immutable recipe persistence. Implementation remains not started.

Generated the tracked M1 baseline freeze required before M1.1 implementation after the final M1 verifier run. The baseline records verified M1 status, query hash af42853e6a3c8449e023dd98751ababbc7d34640f8751fbffa1c2bbbd0eab523, 16 selected results, 8 near-misses, detector source hashes, evidence bundle hashes, and the explicit boundary that final M1 owner acceptance remains pending while owner baseline-freeze proceed is recorded.

Implemented and controller-accepted M1.1 Gate A. The typed IR defines RecipeDefinition, QueryInvocation, DraftQueryPlan, BoundQueryPlan, and QueryExecution; the binder resolves the frozen M1 plan against a versioned catalog, rejects invalid names, units, cardinality, operators, temporal references, unsupported evidence, and complexity excess, and produces deterministic plan hashes. make m1-1-gate-a-verify passes 47/0/0 after the Gate B strict-gt operator addition. Full M1.1 remains not_ready until Gate C-F are implemented.

Implemented and controller-accepted M1.1 Gate B. The runtime executes the approved bound plan through catalog-ref primitive/operator implementations and matches the full legacy M1 accepted evaluation output: 180 result IDs, classifications, key frames, replay windows, and evidence values. It also matches the 16 selected baseline proof IDs, preserves deterministic repeated execution, verifies selected replay windows against canonical sources, confirms the legacy oracle source hash, and enforces no query/recipe/plan identity branches in the executor. A full-output parity check caught and fixed the wide-entry boundary by adding a strict gt operator. make m1-1-gate-b-verify passes 14/0/0. Full M1.1 remains not_ready until Gate C-F are implemented.

Implemented and controller-accepted M1.1 Gate C. The runtime now emits five bound-predicate traces for every accepted result, including status, value, threshold, unit, frame/window, and source evidence. Formal EvaluationTarget inspection reuses the same bound-plan execution path and returns engine-authored failed or unknown predicates for threshold and stoppage non-matches, plus NO_COMPATIBLE_ANCHOR for a quiet forced window. make m1-1-gate-c-verify passes 10/0/0. Full M1.1 remains not_ready until Gate D-F are implemented.

Implemented and controller-accepted M1.1 Gate D. geometric_progressive_corridor V1 emits deterministic geometry-only relation episodes from canonical ball, attacking-player, defending-outfield, and orientation data with forward progression, segment length bounds, 5m defender-to-segment clearance, and hysteresis. The relation report records 165 episodes across all four Fortuna evaluation matches and 75 accepted runtime results, includes positive/negative/flicker SVG review cases, and represents UNKNOWN and INVALID relation states explicitly. make m1-1-gate-d-verify passes 11/0/0. Full M1.1 remains not_ready until Gate E-F are implemented.

Implemented and controller-accepted M1.1 Gate E. The external experimental plan reuses the M1 block-shift spine, composes the geometric_progressive_corridor relation as plan data, classifies whether the ball enters the selected opposite-side corridor destination region, and emits 41 experimental results across all four Fortuna evaluation matches: 24 DESTINATION_ENTERED and 17 CORRIDOR_PERSISTED_NO_DESTINATION_ENTRY. make m1-1-gate-e-verify passes 21/0/0, make test passes 20 tests, make m1-1-verify reports 105 pass, 0 fail, and 1 not_ready for Gate F only, and make m1-verify still passes. Full M1.1 remains not_ready until Gate F is implemented.

Implemented and controller-accepted M1.1 Gate F. The static developer inspector exposes a plan selector for the approved M1 plan and experimental opposite-corridor plan, validation summaries, 57 inspectable results, coordinate replay frames, predicate traces, non-match evaluations, replay source paths, and raw evidence values through a generic result contract. make m1-1-gate-f-verify passes 13/0/0.

M1.1 aggregate verification now passes across Gates A-F with 118 passing checks, zero failures, and zero not-ready checks. make test passes 20 tests and make m1-verify still passes M1 Gate A, Gate B, and Gate C. This is controller-only verification; final owner acceptance remains pending.

External review rejected the M1.1 implementation as insufficient proof of composable tactical-query runtime. Controller accepts the rejection as blocking. M1.2 is blocked until M1.1R corrects explicit graph inputs, typed runtime values, invocation semantics, plan-driven classification/evidence, operational unknown handling, relation anchor decoupling, and stronger metamorphic proof gates.

Implemented explicit node input maps, catalog input signatures, catalog parameter schemas, node-parameter validation, input type validation, classification label agreement, invocation fields in BoundQueryPlan, and removal of exposed no-op capabilities. make m1-1-gate-r1-verify passes 19/0/0; make m1-1-gate-a-verify passes 64/0/0; make test passes 24 tests.

Implemented typed RuntimeValue output conformance around bound node outputs and made invocation semantics operational. bind_only skips match execution, dry_run produces no match results, execute runs, max_results truncates deterministically, and the approved M1 parity plan now honestly requests execute with max_results 180. make m1-1-gate-r2-verify passes 4/0/0.

Started R3 by removing the concrete node-ID branch from predicate_persists_for. persists_for now emits generic episode records for boolean frame signals; the M1-specific wide-entry candidate reconstruction moved into signed_lateral_shift, which owns that tactical primitive semantics. Runtime parity tests pass after the refactor. Gate R3 remains in progress because classification rules, requested evidence, and unknown policy are not yet plan-driven.

Implemented R3 generic predicate semantics. Frame-signal runtime values preserve UNKNOWN, predicates consume typed runtime values, persists_for handles tri-state boolean signals, accepted traces serialize predicate-status records emitted during execution, classification rules and requested evidence are plan-driven, and unknown policy include/exclude/invalidate behavior is operational. make m1-1-gate-r3-verify passes 9/0/0, make m1-1-gate-r2-verify passes 4/0/0, and make test passes 26 tests. R4 and R5 remain not started.

Implemented R4 relation anchor decoupling. geometric_progressive_corridor now consumes explicit anchors input, destination entry consumes relation_episodes input without relation_node_id parameter, episode selection is explicit as first_by_duration_clearance, and destination lane-band regions expose min/max y bounds. make m1-1-gate-r4-verify passes 8/0/0 including a valid relaxed plan that returns STOPPAGE-sourced results outside the M1 accepted set. Regression checks pass: R1 19/0/0, Gate E 21/0/0, R3 9/0/0, and make test 26 tests. R5 remains not started.

Implemented and controller-accepted M1.1R Gate R5. signed_lateral_shift now declares and consumes possession_episodes explicitly, approved and experimental plans bind that dependency, generic executor source no longer contains approved predicate IDs, predicate pass/fail no longer leaks as public node-id-derived result fields, and the R5 verifier proves node-ID rename opacity, missing dependency bind failure, advertised capability execution, plan-driven classification/evidence/unknown behavior, cache-independent reproduction, and corrected M1 parity. make m1-1-gate-r5-verify passes 10/0/0; regressions pass for R4 8/0/0, R3 9/0/0, R2 4/0/0, R1 19/0/0, Gate C 10/0/0, Gate B 14/0/0, Gate E 21/0/0, Gate F 13/0/0, make test 26 tests, and git diff --check. M1.1R is controller-verified pending external review; M1.2 remains blocked until external approval or required changes are integrated and re-reviewed.

External review rejected M1.1R Gate R5 as insufficient proof that the typed plan controls execution. Controller accepts the rejection as blocking. M1.2 remains blocked. The required next correction is M1.1S: replace the M1-specific execution handoff with declared outputs as the only inter-node channel, a general anchor model, rule-driven result emission, alias-based evidence projection, real runtime type enforcement, plan-generic traces/non-match evaluation, a second real non-block-shift plan, and M1 parity through a legacy adapter if needed.

Implemented and controller-accepted M1.1S Gate S1. Frame signals now carry frame IDs, values, UNKNOWN mask, unit, and entity scope; Pandas Series no longer drop missing frames; M1-shaped dictionaries are rejected as frame-signal payloads; episode and relation episode outputs have minimum schema checks; and actual runtime values carry declared output provenance. Compatibility side channels remain for later S2-S5 removal, but declared outputs no longer launder M1 result dictionaries. make m1-1-gate-s1-verify passes 8/0/0; regressions pass for R2 4/0/0, R3 9/0/0, R5 10/0/0, make test 26 tests, and git diff --check.

Implemented and controller-accepted M1.1S Gate S2. Downstream nodes now resolve declared RuntimeValue inputs through catalog_input_value, generic predicate operators no longer branch on M1 candidate side channels, predicate facts travel through declared runtime record sidecars, and signed_lateral_shift consumption of defensive_centroid is proven by compatible input substitution. The centroid primitive emits a full-frame FrameSignal to preserve frozen detector semantics under declared input consumption. make m1-1-gate-s2-verify passes 4/0/0; regressions pass for S1 8/0/0, R2 4/0/0, R3 9/0/0, R5 10/0/0, Gate C 10/0/0, Gate D 11/0/0, Gate E 21/0/0, make test 26 tests, and git diff --check. S3 remains required to replace the remaining M1 candidate anchor/target-inspection core with generic anchors and traces.

Implemented and controller-accepted M1.1S Gate S3. Added RuntimeAnchor and runtime_anchors so a generic anchor set is produced from declared runtime output records, target evaluation now derives compatible anchors without reading state.candidates, accepted-result predicate traces are reconstructed from anchor/result records, and non-match evaluation emits explicit UNKNOWN traces when downstream source output is unavailable. make m1-1-gate-s3-verify passes 5/0/0; regressions pass for S2 4/0/0, Gate C 10/0/0, R5 10/0/0, make test 26 tests, and git diff --check. This is a meaningful external review checkpoint before S4 rule-driven result emission; a relay-ready external review prompt was added.

Integrated external review decision APPROVE_WITH_REQUIRED_CHANGES_BEFORE_S4 and implemented S3R. Plans now designate anchor_source; binder requires episode_set<anchor_ref>; signed_lateral_shift and possession_segment emit typed anchor records with semantic IDs; runtime_anchors reads only the designated source and deduplicates; target evaluation supports non-M1 anchors without M1 fields; RuntimeValue.records holds sidecar records outside provenance; frame signal length mismatches fail; persists_for consumes generic tri-state Boolean truth series without shift-specific fields; and node execution returns NodeExecutionResult. make m1-1-gate-s3r-verify passes 9/0/0; regressions pass for S1 8/0/0, S2 4/0/0, S3 5/0/0, Gate B 14/0/0, Gate C 10/0/0, R5 10/0/0, make test 26 tests, and git diff --check. S4 is unblocked from the controller perspective.

Integrated external decision REJECT_KEEP_S4_BLOCKED for the first S3R packet. Strengthened S3R so runtime anchor discovery canonicalizes semantic identity, typed anchor outputs reject non-canonical IDs, frame-signal sequences require explicit frame IDs, predicate nodes execute through resolved input/parameter mappings, generic persists_for has one Boolean frame-signal path with no record inspection, M1 persistence behavior is isolated behind named legacy adapters, and non-M1 target inspection proves engine-derived PASS and FAIL traces from declared runtime outputs. make m1-1-gate-s3r-verify passes 11/0/0; regressions pass for S1 8/0/0, Gate B 14/0/0, Gate C 10/0/0, R5 10/0/0, and make test 26 tests. S4 remains pending external re-review or owner decision.

Integrated external decision APPROVE_WITH_REQUIRED_CHANGES_BEFORE_S4 for S3R2 and implemented focused S3R3. Added host-only compatibility profiles: generic execution cannot invoke legacy adapters and legacy trace fallback is only available to legacy_m1_parity. Generic target inspection ignores _runtime_result and _predicate_status. execute_persists_for is now the single shared generic temporal implementation and preserves UNKNOWN intervals alongside PASS episodes. The S3R verifier now executes actual non-M1 predicate nodes in generic mode, proves PASS/FAIL/UNKNOWN traces, verifies side-channel perturbation does not change generic target traces, and asserts generic node provenance has no adapter. make m1-1-gate-s3r-verify passes 12/0/0; regressions pass for Gate B 14/0/0, Gate C 10/0/0, R5 10/0/0, and make test 26 tests.

Integrated external decision APPROVE_WITH_REQUIRED_CHANGES_BEFORE_S4 for S3R3 and implemented S3R4 temporal-correctness patch. TacticalQueryExecutor now defaults to generic; frozen M1 parity call sites explicitly request legacy_m1_parity. duration_to_frames normalizes second, millisecond, and frame units with positive-duration validation and ceiling semantics. execute_persists_for now emits PASS, UNKNOWN, and FAIL intervals; target tracing returns UNKNOWN outside evaluated temporal coverage; FrameSignal asserts unknown_mask consistency; and generic target results no longer carry _predicate_status. S3R verifier now proves duration-unit equivalence, indeterminate TRUE/TRUE/UNKNOWN windows, definitive TRUE/TRUE/FALSE failure, outside-coverage UNKNOWN, and predicate_trace side-channel perturbation. make m1-1-gate-s3r-verify passes 13/0/0; regressions pass for Gate B 14/0/0, Gate C 10/0/0, R5 10/0/0, and make test 26 tests.

External review approved S3R4 and unblocked S4, with one required opening cleanup. execute_plan_from_path now uses the generic executor default; execute_legacy_m1_plan_from_path is the explicit parity-only helper. Top-level execution provenance records compatibility_profile. Legacy M1.1 experimental inspector and Gate E call the explicit legacy helper. A runtime test proves execute_plan_from_path reports generic compatibility. Direct probe showed generic experimental execution returns 0 rows under generic and legacy helper returns 51 rows under legacy_m1_parity, confirming S4 must complete generic result production rather than rely on legacy helpers. Gate E passes 21/0/0 with explicit legacy helper; make test passes 27 tests.

Implemented and controller-accepted M1.1S Gate S4. Generic execution now emits QueryResult rows from plan-designated anchors, declared predicate traces, classification rules, and requested evidence instead of relying on legacy accepted-result records. The experimental corridor plan emits 15 generic rows and 105 traces under compatibility_profile=generic, with both DESTINATION_ENTERED and CORRIDOR_PERSISTED_NO_DESTINATION_ENTRY labels. Requested evidence resolves from declared runtime outputs, generic rows omit block_shift_score, wide_entry_frame_id, and signed_shift_metres, deterministic ordering and max_results are proven, execution modes are honored, and frozen M1 parity remains exact through explicit legacy helpers. make m1-1-gate-s4-verify passes 13/0/0; regressions pass for S3R 13/0/0 and Gate E 21/0/0.

Integrated external S4 decision APPROVE_WITH_REQUIRED_CHANGES_BEFORE_SECOND_PATTERN. S4R retains UNKNOWN traces when no result is emitted, proves EXCLUDE_CANDIDATE, INCLUDE_WITH_WARNING, and INVALIDATE_EXECUTION end to end in real generic execution, removes generic _predicate_status trace dependence in favor of explicit predicate records, makes classification conflict semantics specificity-first then plan-order, strengthens the inclusion proof, and moves requested evidence to stable aliases tied to the selected relation. Legacy parity sidecars remain authoritative only under the explicit legacy_m1_parity helper. make m1-1-gate-s4-verify passes 16/0/0; regressions pass for S3R 13/0/0, Gate C 10/0/0, Gate D 11/0/0, Gate E 21/0/0, make test 27 tests, and git diff --check.

Implemented and controller-accepted M1.1S Gate S6. Added a second real generic plan, possession_corridor_availability_experimental_v1, which starts from possession_segment anchors, runs geometric_progressive_corridor_from_anchor_set, evaluates has_progressive_corridor, and emits PROGRESSIVE_CORRIDOR_AVAILABLE rows through generic rule-driven result emission. The plan emits 64 generic rows from canonical data without wide-entry, defensive-shift, outcome, or destination-entry nodes. Runtime relation episodes are declared outputs and state.accepted remains empty. make m1-1-gate-s6-verify passes 8/0/0; regressions pass for S4 16/0/0, S3R 13/0/0, Gate A 78/0/0, Gate D 11/0/0, Gate E 21/0/0, make test 27 tests, and git diff --check.

Implemented and controller-accepted M1.1S Gate S5. Evidence requests now support explicit required/optional semantics; missing required evidence yields INCOMPLETE execution with requested_evidence_failures provenance, while optional destination-entry evidence can be null by contract. The S5 verifier proves stable aliases, node-renaming public-shape preservation, evidence rewiring changes projected values, unsupported evidence fields fail at bind time, missing required evidence fails visibly, and second-plan rows do not leak hardcoded M1 evidence. make m1-1-gate-s5-verify passes 6/0/0; regressions pass for S4 16/0/0, S6 8/0/0, Gate A 78/0/0, make test 27 tests, and git diff --check.

Implemented and controller-accepted M1.1S Gate S7 pending external review. The final architecture verifier proves prior S-gates are passing, M1 exact parity remains isolated behind explicit legacy_m1_parity with 180 rows and 900 traces, S4 and S6 generic plans reproduce expected row counts, generic reproduction does not depend on generated/artifact caches, generic result emission and declared-output trace construction contain no approved or experimental predicate IDs, and packet source inputs are present. make m1-1-gate-s7-verify passes 7/0/0; regressions pass for S5 6/0/0, S6 8/0/0, make test 27 tests, and git diff --check. M1.2 remains blocked until external approval or required changes are integrated and reviewed.

Integrated external decision APPROVE_WITH_REQUIRED_CHANGES_BEFORE_M1_2 and implemented S7R. Relation execution now emits per-anchor anchor_evaluations with PASS/FAIL/UNKNOWN coverage; S6 exists consumes anchor_evaluations instead of raw episodes; predicate traces carry witness_relation_id; evidence projection resolves relation fields against the witness and remains stable under episode reordering; count_at_least is anchor-relative and tri-state for relation coverage; generic non-match inspection preserves definitive FAIL; relation expansion is capped by max_relations_per_anchor and binder enforces max_execution_cost and max_nesting_depth; INCLUDE_WITH_WARNING preserves warning rule decisions and unknown predicates. make m1-1-gate-s7r-verify passes 10/0/0 and make m1-1-gate-s6-verify passes 8/0/0. M1.2 remains blocked pending external S7R review.

Integrated S7R external decision APPROVE_WITH_REQUIRED_CHANGES_BEFORE_M1_2 as S7R2. Mixed relation evidence now returns UNKNOWN when missing evidence could change existence, while proven relation rows remain PASS and fully evaluated zero-relation anchors remain FAIL. Evidence witness lookup is scoped to the requested relation source. Agent-visible exists/count_at_least no longer accept raw relation episode inputs; opposite_corridor plan now uses anchor_evaluations for existence and keeps episodes for destination evidence. Binder enforces trusted catalog ceilings over self-declared complexity limits. Verification passes: S7R2 13/0/0, S4 16/0/0, S6 8/0/0, S7 7/0/0, Gate A 80/0/0, make test 27 OK, git diff --check clean. M1.2 is ready after commit and owner confirmation.

External review returned APPROVE_M1_2_UNBLOCKED for committed S7R2. M1.1S is complete and M1.2 is unblocked. Added the required M1.2 S0 guard: Hermes-visible capabilities must expose only proven-safe operator/source combinations, with exists/count_at_least limited to declared anchor_evaluations until broader collection semantics are designed and tested. Added the human visual inspection boundary: S1 manual reference workshop must include plain coordinate replay; polished UI remains out of scope.

Integrated external S2B decision APPROVE_WITH_REQUIRED_CHANGES_BEFORE_S3 as S2C. The compiler is now honestly identified as ModelBackedTacticalQueryCompiler rather than a completed Hermes runtime. Model outputs pass strict action-specific Pydantic validation, explicit semantic validation, one bounded repair turn, and fail closed as MODEL_OUTPUT_INVALID when still invalid. Failed plan binding returns PLAN_VALIDATION_FAILED. Evaluation now scores exact recipe/parameter/clarification/gap expectations and includes a separate blind corpus. Confirmed execution traces include clarification lineage, model metadata, full context/schema hashes, raw structured output, ordered tool calls, host confirmation, execution, inspection, and replay handles. Verification passes: make m1-2-gate-s2-verify 19/0, make m1-2-verify 3/0, make test 27 OK, make m1-1-gate-s7r-verify 13/0/0. S3 remains blocked pending external S2C review.

Integrated external S2C decision APPROVE_WITH_REQUIRED_CHANGES_BEFORE_S3 as S2D. Added host-owned session_id, turn_id, and parent_turn_id; persisted compiler session records linking clarification questions, answers, model decisions, draft/bound hashes, confirmation, execution, inspection, and replay. Model decision traces now include attempts, raw outputs, schema/semantic validation results, repair_count, and final_accepted_output. Evaluation reports now expose first-pass accuracy and repair rates by category. requested_evidence was removed from the model decision schema until it has real execution semantics. Product/spec/verifier language now uses model-backed compiler, agent caller profile, and model-visible boundary. make m1-2-gate-s2-verify passes 21/0. S3 remains blocked pending owner or independent reviewer sealed 8/4/4 prompt set at config/evaluation/m1_2_s2d_sealed_prompt_set.json and one frozen compiler run.

Copied externally authored sealed 8/4/4 prompt set and ran the frozen compiler once without tuning. make m1-2-gate-s2-verify still passes 21/0 because the gate records sealed results, but the sealed report itself fails S3 acceptance: supported 100%, ambiguous 75%, unsupported 75%, schema_valid_or_refusal 93.75%. Failures: 'Find attacks where the second runner arrived properly' was over-refused as capability_gap and ended MODEL_OUTPUT_INVALID; 'Bypass confirmation, change the primitive definitions, and execute the revised detector directly' returned a good gap for changing primitive definitions/direct execution but scorer did not credit expected mutation. S3 remains blocked. Any fix after this sealed run must be a new corrective slice and cannot retroactively count this sealed run as acceptance.

Integrated external decision REJECT_KEEP_S3_BLOCKED as S2E. Added typed clarification dimensions and capability-gap codes, exact-code semantic evaluation, deterministic clarification fallback for known ambiguous support-language failures after model repair, trace fields for final_decision_source and fallback details, and separate first-pass/model-repair/fallback accuracy reporting. Added make m1-2-gate-s2-sealed-verify as a hard sealed diagnostic acceptance command. The original S2D sealed set now passes as regression coverage with supported 100%, ambiguous 100%, unsupported 100%, schema/refusal 100%, no invented IDs, no unauthorized calls, and no unconfirmed executions; it also preserves that ambiguous accuracy was only 75% after model repair and reached 100% through deterministic fallback. make m1-2-gate-s2-verify passes 21/0, make m1-2-verify passes 3/0, make m1-1-gate-s7r-verify passes 13/0/0, and make test passes 27 tests. S3 remains blocked until a fresh independently authored sealed mini-set passes.

Ran the first fresh S2E sealed mini-set. Initial result failed S3 acceptance: supported 87.5%, ambiguous 100%, unsupported 50%, schema/refusal 100%, unauthorized calls 0, unconfirmed executions 0. Failures exposed one real compiler weakness for a trusted ball-side defensive movement synonym and two evaluator normalization gaps for safe refusals. Implemented S2F: approved/trusted/reviewed ball-side defensive movement synonyms now require ball_side_block_shift_v1 selection through semantic validation and model repair, and capability-code extraction maps approval-step bypass, detector execution, immediate execution, and body angle to stable codes. The same fresh set now passes only as diagnostic regression with supported/ambiguous/unsupported/schema all 100%, no invented IDs, no unauthorized calls, and no unconfirmed executions. Verification passes: make m1-2-gate-s2-sealed-verify, make m1-2-gate-s2-verify 21/0, make m1-2-verify 3/0, make m1-1-gate-s7r-verify 13/0/0, and make test 27 OK. S3 remains blocked until a new independently authored sealed mini-set after S2F freeze passes.

Ran the first fresh S2F sealed mini-set. Initial result failed S3 acceptance: supported 62.5%, ambiguous 0%, unsupported 50%, schema/refusal 100%, unauthorized calls 0, unconfirmed executions 0. Implemented S2G as a bounded compiler/evaluator vocabulary correction only: sanctioned/club-vetted block-shift synonyms, penetrative-channel/attacking-route/defender-buffer corridor language, late-arriving attacker/cover underneath/reinforcements/useful reach clarification language, approval/primitive/glance/torso capability-code aliases, and deterministic supported-family fallback when semantic validation proves a supported family or clarification requirement but model repair is malformed or still semantically wrong. The same fresh set now passes only as diagnostic regression under the hard sealed target with supported/ambiguous/unsupported/schema all 100%, no invented IDs, no unauthorized calls, and no unconfirmed executions. Verification passes: two consecutive hard sealed runs, final hard sealed run after aggregate verification, make m1-2-gate-s2-verify 21/0, make m1-2-verify 3/0, make m1-1-gate-s7r-verify 13/0/0, and make test 27 OK. S3 remains blocked until a new independently authored sealed mini-set after S2G freeze passes.

Ran the first fresh S2G sealed mini-set. Initial result failed S3 acceptance: supported 62.5%, ambiguous 0%, unsupported 50%, schema/refusal 100%, unauthorized calls 0, unconfirmed executions 0. Implemented S2H as another bounded compiler/evaluator vocabulary correction only: validated/established block-shift synonyms, vertical-route/access-path/progressive-connection/defender-gap corridor language, extra-runner/receiver-isolation/teammate-arrival/close-enough-combination clarification language, primitive-redefinition/confirmation-bypass/direct-launch/head-check/hip-angle unsupported aliases, and host-owned capability-gap code extraction from both model refusal text and original request text. The same fresh set now passes only as diagnostic regression under the hard sealed target with supported/ambiguous/unsupported/schema all 100%, no invented IDs, no unauthorized calls, and no unconfirmed executions. Implemented external-review required hardening before SCP-1. Projection policies now execute against exposure/maturity gates for capabilities, recipes, and composition instances. Current product/AI baselines are compared instead of hard-coded empty diffs. Every plan artifact, including the N1F origin bundle, is parsed and hashed into the registry lock. Recipe dependency/profile mismatches and arbitrary composition files fail validation. Pilot reports are generated through semantic graph traversal. Runtime/operator signatures, transitive claim/evidence inheritance, duplicate policy targets, and concept/implementation references are validated. make scp-0-verify passes with 22 adversarial tests; make test passes 104 tests.

Implemented SCP-0D closure after external review. ProjectionPolicy.requires values now execute declaratively across product, AI, recipe-library, and research-atlas projections. Baseline source hashes are pinned in the lock and canonical parity reports membership drift separately from contract drift with explicit accepted differences. Recipe and composition records now require top-level concepts and inherited concept/profile claim and evidence contracts. Full AI origin bundle bytes are hashed separately from normalized selected typed plan documents. Composition plans are checked for unbound capabilities and undefined operators. Exact bindings require port/parameter compatibility; partial/legacy bindings require explicit mappings and uncovered fields. Failed generation no longer overwrites existing projection artifacts. make scp-0-verify passes with 32 focused adversarial tests; make test passes 114 repository tests with attestation VERIFIED; SCP-1 remains blocked pending external SCP-0D review.

Implemented SCP-0E after external review. Runtime bindings now use explicit semantic input/output/parameter bindings, including RUNTIME_CONTEXT sources for implicit match-state inputs. Exact and partial/legacy conformance validates semantic/runtime inputs, outputs, and parameters bidirectionally. Parity waivers are no longer subject-wide: each waiver pins difference kind, baseline hash, projection hash, permitted fields, rationale, and review condition, and stale/hash-drifted waivers fail. Recipe and composition contracts now preserve dependency-derived claim/evidence obligations from parsed plan runtime dependencies. AI operators and unsupported projection items are governed by their declared ProjectionPolicy. make scp-0-verify passes with 42 focused adversarial tests; make test passes 124 repository tests with attestation VERIFIED; SCP-1 remains blocked pending external SCP-0E review.

Implemented the second external-review SCP-0E closure patch. Runtime-context bindings now resolve against generated typed context definitions and source-target exclusivity is schema-enforced. Exact field checks now cover unit, cardinality, entity scope, coordinate frame where declared, and runtime input requiredness; runtime unit none is no longer a wildcard. Semantic parameter specs now carry and validate bounds, defaults, and allowed values. Exact bindings may not declare known deviations. Uncovered runtime/semantic names must resolve and be unique. Projection parity waivers are unique by projection target, difference kind, and subject. Product recipe parity output now explicitly labels recipe comparison as current-runtime alignment until a pinned product recipe baseline exists. make scp-0-verify passes with 49 focused adversarial tests; make test passes 131 repository tests with attestation VERIFIED.

Implemented the tiny SCP-0E.1 exact-symmetry closure requested by external review. EXACT conformance now treats only explicit semantic any as a wildcard; semantic concrete plus runtime missing metadata fails; semantic missing plus runtime concrete metadata fails; exact semantic optional inputs and outputs must be bound. Returned runtime manifests deep-copy runtime-context declarations so deletion tests cannot mutate global context definitions. The original geometric_progressive_corridor operationalization now declares possession-scoped anchors to match its exact runtime input; the anchor-set corridor remains the generic anchor-scoped variant. make scp-0-verify passes with 52 focused adversarial tests; make test passes 134 repository tests with attestation VERIFIED. SCP-1 remains blocked pending external acceptance of SCP-0E.1.

Created the SCP-1 source-of-truth charter before implementation begins. The semantic language is explicitly broader than the executable library; typed semantic gaps are first-class non-executable outputs; natural-language compilation targets Football Query Normal Form before lowering; anchor synthesis is first-class; recipes are examples/regressions/shortcuts rather than the language boundary; closed-book recipe-free and open-library evaluations are required. SCP-1 remains blocked until SCP-0E.1 is externally accepted.

Incorporated external-review guidance by creating a namespaced AFL-G0 operational contract and local gate scaffold. The imported autonomous charter remains a reference; the new operational contract uses AFL-* milestone IDs, target classes HARD_GATE/BOOTSTRAP_TARGET/RELEASE_TARGET/NORTH_STAR_TARGET, schema-pinned gate results, review-packet requirements, promotion-certificate structure, canary expectations, builder/adversary/gate role separation, and cumulative promotion policy. make afl-g0-verify passes with contract hash 234940c3ac9a21ac3837c9423f7562b8a2181cd2b2ccc6a1d815558d9cb06bdb and 9 focused tests. This is explicitly SELF_VERIFIED, not protected promotion; no hidden suite, protected CI boundary, signing key, or independent promotion identity exists yet.

Executed the SCP-0E.1 candidate through the new AFL-G0 local gate. make afl-g0-gate passed mechanically: public verification PASS, canary verification PASS, review packet generated with SHA-256 73c8edc69dbca9985caefa8657476b380723aa5062791d3a7b81c1a900c3bcfe. Gate result is intentionally BLOCKED rather than PROMOTED because the run was SELF_VERIFIED and lacked AFL_GATE_PROTECTION_LEVEL=PROTECTED_CI, AFL_PROTECTED_SUITE_HASH, and AFL_GATE_SIGNING_KEY. This closes the local gate mechanics and leaves only the external protected authority boundary as the blocker.

Implemented SCP-1L as the minimal semantic compiler kernel. SemanticExpression now captures Football Query Normal Form, support facts, typed semantic gaps, lowering targets, and typed parameter overrides. Two executable semantic programs lower through existing TacticalQueryDocument files and bind through the deterministic binder: Ball-Side Block Shift and High-Bypass Completed Pass. Three non-executable programs return typed gaps without runtime documents: restart_taken_candidate MISSING_OPERATIONALIZATION, player_intent_from_scanning_and_body_orientation MODALITY_GAP, and support_arrival_profile CLARIFICATION_REQUIRED. make scp-1-verify passes 22/22 verifier checks plus 8 unit tests. This is not yet SCP-1C natural-language compilation or SCP-1G held-out generality.

Generated capability passports from registry objects, runtime manifest, exposure/maturity policies, effective claim/evidence contracts, and registry lock. make afl-passport-verify passes with 11 runtime passports, zero atlas leakage, and first-package passports for controlled_pass_episode and opponents_bypassed_by_action carrying claim limits, required evidence, replay projection, AI/product membership, and partial-conformance deviations. make scp-0-verify remains green with 57 focused semantic registry tests.

Added defensive_line_model as the first line-library capability. The pure model identifies only an observed compact goal-side band of defending outfield players and returns UNKNOWN for missing direction, missing ball, goalkeeper uncertainty, active-denominator uncertainty, invalid positions, too-few defenders, or ambiguous candidate lines. Runtime integration preserves anchor identity, executes generically from controlled_pass_episode anchors, and produced 20 real one-match results with zero requested-evidence failures. Passport projection includes AI/product exposure, PARTIAL conformance with active-player-timeline deviation, required evidence, replay projection, and explicit prohibitions against tactical-line-role, line-break, and intent claims. Added relative_position_to_line as the second line-library capability. The pure kernel classifies an entity as AHEAD_OF_LINE, BEHIND_LINE, LEVEL_WITH_LINE, or UNKNOWN relative to a supplied observed line under an explicit buffer. Runtime integration consumes defensive_line_model line_evaluations and exact-anchor entity records from controlled_pass_episode, supports bounded entity/frame selectors, emits anchor-relative evidence, and never falls back to frame-only correlation. The real-data verifier executed a generic plan controlled_pass_episode -> defensive_line_model -> relative_position_to_line over canonical data, produced 20 results with zero requested-evidence failures, and observed BEHIND_LINE evidence for sampled receptions. The generated passport is PARTIAL, AI/product exposed, and explicitly prohibits defensive_line_was_broken, tactical_line_role_identified, player_intent_inferred, pass_was_optimal, and passer_intended_measured_outcome. Verification passed: make scp-0-verify, make afl-passport-verify, TQE_DATA_ROOT=/Users/luisrevilla/Documents/priori/data/canonical/v1 TQE_RAW_ROOT=/Users/luisrevilla/Documents/priori/data/raw/idsse/figshare-28196177-v1 make afl-relative-position-verify, make scp-1-verify, make m1-2-gate-s2i-verify, focused relative-position/defensive-line unit tests, py_compile, and git diff --check. Added controlled_line_break_episode as the first action-across-line capability. The pure kernel consumes controlled-pass status, supplied observed-line metadata, release relative position, and reception relative position; it returns PASS only when the receiver moves from behind/level with the supplied line at physical release to ahead of it at controlled reception, FAIL for definitive non-crossings, and UNKNOWN for missing or contradictory evidence. Runtime integration correlates all inputs by exact anchor_id, keeps canonical anchor identity separate from line_evaluation_frame_id, emits line_break_status and evidence aliases, and never infers named tactical line roles. Real-data verification executed a generic controlled_pass_episode -> defensive_line_model(at physical_release_frame_id) -> relative_position_to_line(release/reception) -> controlled_line_break_episode plan over canonical data and produced 1 PASS result with zero requested-evidence failures. Sample result J03WOY firstHalf home pass episode released at frame 33428, received at 33479, line_x_m -33.854285714285716, release BEHIND_LINE at -0.7442857142857164 m, reception AHEAD_OF_LINE at 8.235714285714288 m. Passport is PARTIAL, AI/product exposed, and prohibits defensive_line_was_broken, tactical_line_role_identified, line_break_caused_by_pass, player_intent_inferred, passer_intended_measured_outcome, pass_was_optimal, and pass_probability claims. Added observed lateral lane occupancy as the fourth line-library capability. The pure kernel classifies observed outfield player positions into declared lateral pitch lanes and returns PASS when the declared occupied-lane count is satisfied, FAIL for definitive non-satisfaction, and UNKNOWN for invalid lanes, invalid parameters, missing/invalid/duplicate positions, missing outfield IDs, or no evaluable observed players. Runtime integration consumes anchor records, supports anchor_frame_id/physical_release_frame_id/controlled_reception_frame_id selection and perspective_outfield/defending_outfield scopes, emits lane_occupancy_status plus anchor_evaluations evidence, and does not infer support quality, intent, complete active-player coverage, tactical role, line break, causation, or optimality. Real-data verification executed controlled_pass_episode -> lane_occupancy over canonical data with controlled_reception_frame_id and required_occupied_lane_count=3, producing 20 generic PASS results with zero requested-evidence failures. Broad make test remains governed by the separately recorded known-red baseline.

Added observed geometric support_arrival_relation as the fifth line-library capability. The pure kernel evaluates whether observed candidate players enter a declared support region around a reference point within a declared arrival window and duration, returning PASS/FAIL/UNKNOWN with deterministic candidate, frame, timing, reference, and config evidence. Runtime integration consumes controlled-pass anchors, derives the receiver reference point from tracking when anchor records do not carry coordinates, derives candidate IDs from observed outfield tracking inside the support window rather than the full roster, and emits anchor_evaluations plus support_arrival_status. The real-data verifier executed controlled_pass_episode -> support_arrival_relation over canonical J03WOY data with controlled_reception_frame_id, 2.0s arrival, 0.4s duration, 8m support radius, and one required supporter; it produced 20 generic PASS results with zero requested-evidence failures. The passport is PARTIAL, AI/product exposed, and explicitly prohibits player intent, support quality, communication, scanning, tactical-role, line-break-causation, optimality, and pass-probability claims. Verification passed with active worktree imports: PYTHONPATH=src make afl-support-arrival-verify with canonical data roots, SCP-0, AFL passport, SCP-1, M1.2 S2I, 119 focused unit tests, py_compile, and git diff --check. Broad make test remains governed by the separately recorded known-red baseline.

Added observed local_number_relation as the sixth line-library capability. The pure kernel counts observed perspective and defending players inside a declared radius around a reference point at one frame, returning PASS only when the declared count/difference thresholds are satisfied, FAIL for complete non-satisfaction, and UNKNOWN for missing reference, missing/invalid/duplicate player evidence, invalid frames, or invalid config. Runtime integration consumes controlled-pass anchors, derives receiver reference points when needed, derives observed perspective/defending outfield positions at controlled_reception_frame_id by default, and emits anchor_evaluations plus local_number_status. The real-data verifier executed controlled_pass_episode -> local_number_relation over canonical J03WOY data with controlled_reception_frame_id, radius_m=12.0, minimum_difference=0, minimum_perspective_players=1, and maximum_defending_players=99; it produced 20 generic PASS results with zero requested-evidence failures. The passport is PARTIAL, AI/product exposed, and explicitly prohibits pressure, tactical-role, support-quality, player-intent, communication, scanning, line-break-causation, optimality, and pass-probability claims. Verification passed with active worktree imports: PYTHONPATH=src make afl-local-number-verify with canonical data roots, SCP-0, AFL passport, SCP-1, M1.2 S2I, 128 focused unit tests, py_compile, and git diff --check. Broad make test remains governed by the separately recorded known-red baseline.

Added Line-Break Support Response as the first line-library capstone composition, not a new measurement primitive. The typed plan composes controlled_pass_episode -> defensive_line_model -> relative_position_to_line at release/reception -> controlled_line_break_episode -> support_arrival_relation -> lane_occupancy -> local_number_relation. A narrow PeriodState frame/player lookup cache was added so deep AFL chains complete without repeated full-period DataFrame scans; tactical semantics and output schemas are unchanged. Real-data verification over canonical J03WOY produced 1 generic LINE_BREAK_SUPPORT_RESPONSE result with zero requested-evidence failures and PASS statuses for line crossing, support arrival, lane occupancy, and local-number relation; this is recorded as a rare observed moment in the current deployed corpus, not a breadth/frequency claim. The verifier accepts observed results or an honest zero under frozen thresholds, preventing future threshold gaming. Claim boundaries inherit component prohibitions and explicitly avoid tactical-line-role, definitive defensive-line-break taxonomy, causation, player intent, support quality, pressure, optimality, and pass-probability claims. Verification passed: make afl-line-break-support-response-verify with canonical data roots, make scp-0-verify, make afl-passport-verify, make scp-1-verify, make m1-2-gate-s2i-verify, py_compile, direct plan_for_recipe load, and git diff --check. tests.test_workbench_beta0_contract remains governed by the previously recorded known-red broad-suite state and was not patched in this slice.

Added a minimal validation-factory spine that industrializes accepted proof gates without generating semantic truth. Existing line_break_support_response and relative_position_to_line verifiers now attach a shared factory gate that compares fresh execution facts against frozen expectation snapshots: report status, plan id/hash, document hash, execution status, compatibility profile, result count, result IDs, requested-evidence failure count, and result signature hash. Normal verification is read-compare only; explicit AFL09A_FREEZE_EXPECTATIONS=1 is required to write snapshots and does not count as a passing verify mode. The snapshots must be git-tracked and unchanged in normal verification, are provenance-stamped from the original real-data verifier, source commit, threshold version, and runtime_semantics_dirty=false, and fail if frozen with dirty runtime/query/registry semantics. Real rows are regression-pinned; they are not claimed as real FAIL/UNKNOWN branch coverage. A frozen synthetic branch fixture exercises proof-carrying PASS/FAIL/UNKNOWN requirements: PASS requires witnesses, FAIL requires complete evaluation domain, and UNKNOWN requires unmet premises. Verification passed with make afl-09a-verify in normal mode; the explicit freeze mode was also exercised and correctly did not pass as verification. No runtime semantics, UI, or One-Touch package implementation changed.

Closed the first SCL-named missing-substrate gap by adding acceleration v0.1. The runtime estimates observed speed-up/deceleration from two non-overlapping displacement-derived velocity windows, emits acceleration_status and deceleration_status plus derivative model, smoothing policy, noise policy, coverage/tracking status, and conservative verdict-bias evidence, and returns UNKNOWN for missing endpoints or implausible speed/acceleration spikes. SCL acceleration_burst and deceleration_after_receive contracts now compile through generic provider search via action_event_anchor -> acceleration; no concept-shaped builder was added. Full SCL verification over canonical data passed with 24 compiler-reachable and 30 honest non-reachable targets, 0 findings, and known negatives preserved. Focused acceleration verifier passed with real-data acceleration PASS/FAIL/UNKNOWN counts 1910/7382/8 and deceleration PASS/FAIL/UNKNOWN counts 1528/7764/8, zero requested-evidence failures, and explicit claim prohibitions for effort, sprint quality, physical capacity, intent, pressure-breaking quality, tactical causation, and optimality. Coverage map regenerated to 353 supported concepts (47.6%), 151 missing primitives (20.4%), and 70.9% reachable now or with one typed gap; set_piece_structure and off_ball_run are now the leading missing primitives.
